circle.data-narration: An ecosystem is a complex network of living organisms and non-living components that interact with each other in a specific environment.

g.text: Interconnectedness: Living and non-living components are interconnected.
Interdependence: Each component depends on others for survival.
Self-regulation: Ecosystems regulate their own processes to maintain balance.
Diversity: Ecosystems contain a variety of species and habitats.

rect.data-narration: Terrestrial ecosystem: land-based ecosystems (forests, grasslands, deserts, etc.)
Freshwater ecosystem: water-based ecosystems (rivers, lakes, wetlands, etc.)
Marine ecosystem: ocean-based ecosystems (coral reefs, kelp forests, open ocean, etc.)
Aerial ecosystem: atmosphere-based ecosystems (atmosphere, clouds, wind, etc.)

line.data-narration: These ecosystems are interconnected and interdependent.
Each ecosystem provides unique services to humans.

rect.data-narration: Provisioning services: food, water, timber, and other resources.
Regulating services: climate regulation, air and water filtration, soil formation, etc.

line.data-narration: Recreational services: tourism, recreation, and relaxation.
Cultural services: spiritual, aesthetic, and cultural values.

g.text: Sustainability refers to the ability of an ecosystem to maintain its structures, functions, and processes over time without degrading its resources.

line.data-narration: It involves managing human activities in a way that minimizes harm to the environment and ensures the long-term health of ecosystems.

g.g: <circle cx="50%" cy="75%" r="40" fill="#F7F7F7" data-narration="Biodiversity refers to the variety of different species of plants, animals, and microorganisms that live in an ecosystem or on Earth as a whole."] Genetic diversity: variation within species. Species diversity: variation between species. Ecosystem diversity: variation within ecosystems.
